title: ISSRE 2026 Keynotes Announced
summary: We are proud to announce the distinguished Keynote Speakers for ISSRE 2026 in Cyprus: Prof. Don Syme (GitHub Next), Prof. Paolo Tonella (USI), and Prof. Laurie Williams (NCSU).
content:
We are excited to share the distinguished Keynote Speakers for the upcoming IEEE International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering (ISSRE 2026) in Paphos, Cyprus:

Prof. Don Syme (GitHub Next & King's College London)
  • Title: The Agentic Repository Automation Revolution
  • Overview: Exploring Continuous AI (CAI) in repository automation, AI-assisted code improvement, performance engineering, and automated formal verification.

Prof. Paolo Tonella (Università della Svizzera italiana - USI)
  • Title: AI Testing: What have we learned? Where are we heading?
  • Overview: Analyzing the exponential growth of deep learning model testing, test adequacy assessment, model-level vs. system-level failures, and quality evaluation of LLM-generated code.

Prof. Laurie Williams (North Carolina State University - NCSU)
  • Title: Dependable Software, Untrusted Parts: Security Research for the Software Supply Chain
  • Overview: Examining software supply chain security, lessons from the NSF S3C2 center, software provenance, vulnerability intelligence, developer environment security, and trustworthy software production systems in the era of AI-assisted development.

Join us in Cyprus this October to attend these keynotes and meet the speakers!

title: Hosting Proposals for ISSRE 2029 Now Open
summary: The ISSRE Steering Committee invites proposals from academic institutions or organizations to host the 40th edition of the symposium in 2029.
content:
The IEEE International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering (ISSRE) Steering Committee is officially inviting proposals to host the 40th edition of the symposium in the fall of 2029. Hosting ISSRE is a unique opportunity to bring together leading global researchers and industry experts in software quality and dependability to your local region.

Interested organizers are encouraged to review the Hosting Guidelines under the Documents section and contact the Steering Committee Chair. Preliminary proposals should detail potential venue locations, proposed dates, local organizing committee chairs, accessibility, and tentative budgets.

title: ISSRE 2027 Co-Chairs Announced
summary: The PC Co-Chairs (Weiyi Shang, Naghmeh Ivaki) and Industry Co-Chairs (Nuno Silva, Guangtai Liang, Yajin Zhou) for ISSRE 2027 in Macau have been officially appointed.
content:
Following the selection of the General Co-Chairs for ISSRE 2027, the Steering Committee has finalized the appointments for the Program Committee (PC) Co-Chairs and Industry Co-Chairs for the upcoming symposium.

Program Committee (PC) Co-Chairs:
  • Weiyi Shang (University of Waterloo, Canada)
  • Naghmeh Ivaki (University of Coimbra, Portugal)

Industry Co-Chairs:
  • Nuno Silva (Critical Software, Portugal)
  • Guangtai Liang (Huawei Cloud Computing Technologies, China)
  • Yajin Zhou (Zhejiang University, China)

These leading researchers and practitioners will guide the technical tracks and industrial sessions to ensure a high-quality, impactful program for the 38th edition of the symposium.

title: ISSRE 2027 to be Hosted in Macau
summary: We are thrilled to officially announce Macau as the host city for the 38th edition of the symposium in 2027, with General Co-Chairs Tao Zhang and Barbara Gallina.
content:
The ISSRE Steering Committee is pleased to share that Macau has been selected to host the IEEE International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering (ISSRE) in 2027. We are also delighted to announce the General Co-Chairs for this edition: Tao Zhang (Macau University of Science and Technology, Macau) and Barbara Gallina (Mälardalen University, Sweden).

Macau offers a rich cultural heritage, outstanding conference facilities, and is a major hub connecting researchers across Asia and globally. The local organizing committee has already begun preliminary planning under their leadership. Official dates, venue details, and the call for papers will be announced on the upcoming 2027 website later this year.
